methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

data.narrative:
Standard Subsea, a seed-stage ocean and maritime technology company founded by Christopher Amidon, has raised $10M+ to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comp universe of three private peers in the frontier ocean-tech sector—Clippership, NetworkOcean, and Thalassa Robotics—with no public comparables identified.

The Stage-Progression method is the applicable triangulation approach for this seed-stage asset. Using total capital raised of $10M as a valuation proxy, a standard 2.5x step-up multiplier to the next funding stage yields an implied valuation of $25M. This method reflects typical Seed-to-Series A value progression within the frontier maritime technology space, where public benchmarks remain unavailable.

The analysis triangulation supports a valuation range of $21M to $29M, with a median point of $25M. The relatively tight band reflects the single applicable methodology; key uncertainties center on Standard Subsea's specific unit economics, competitive positioning relative to the three private peers, revenue traction (if any), and the broader market appetite for frontier subsea solutions. Given the seed stage and limited methodology diversity, investors should treat this range as preliminary pending deeper operational diligence and market validation.
